name: Deliberate Practice — 45 min technique focus
description: Pick one specific weakness. Warm up separately. Baseline it. Drill it deliberately. Rest. Contextualize. Reflect. The full Ericsson protocol in a session-friendly shape.

items.label: Warm up (any warmup you like)
items.instruction: Get loose. Long tones, gentle scales, whatever wakes your hands up. Not counted as deliberate work — just prep. Keep it easy; deliberate practice is metabolically expensive and needs a warm system.

items.label: Identify today's specific weakness
items.instruction: One thing. In one sentence. Something you can measure. Examples: 'clean 16ths across the neck at 100 BPM,' 'the interval jump from bar 12 to 13,' 'the m7♭5 arpeggio in every key at 80 BPM.' Write it down before you continue.

items.label: Baseline it — 10 attempts, count the clean ones
items.instruction: Play the target 10 times at your intended tempo. Count how many were clean (every note right, no tension, no hesitation). If 8+, you're not in the challenge zone — pick something harder. If 0-2, back off — pick something easier. Sweet spot: 3-7 out of 10.

items.label: Deliberate work — 20 minutes
items.instruction: Loop the target. Slow it down if needed. Isolate the smallest chunk that's still failing. Vary rhythm and articulation. Every attempt: was that clean? If no — what specifically went wrong? Fix that one thing on the next attempt. Full concentration; no phone.

items.label: Rest — full stop
items.guidanceText: Walk away. Get water. Stretch. Do not touch the instrument. Your brain consolidates during this window; treat it as part of the work.

items.label: Contextualize — 8 minutes
items.instruction: Play the target in its full context. If it was a passage from a piece, play the whole section. If it was a technical pattern, weave it into some improvisation. Does it hold up when the surrounding music asks things of you?

items.label: Reflect — 2 minutes
items.instruction: What worked? What didn't? Rate today's clean-count vs the baseline. What's tomorrow's target — same thing again, or a different weakness? Write it down.
